schema_version: v0 — migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: Session-continuity records — the state of work at a session boundary, so the next session resumes without re-deriving.
why: Sessions are bounded by context limits; continuity must live in a durable, shareable artifact, not in memory of a conversation that won't survive the boundary. A slate is knowledge the SYSTEM would lose (not just CC's personal memory). Written by skill:/saveslate, read by skill:/loadslate.
owner: the slates store owns writes; the single commons emit chokepoint validates every record against the template below (Law 6).

template.author: who wrote it (CC | Akien)
template.in_flight: what is actively being worked, and its state
template.open_threads: array — unresolved questions, IOUs, things to verify (each honest about my-read vs ratified)

notes_on_the_store: A slate is a best-guess snapshot at a boundary, carrying its open threads — not a claim anything is finished (learning-loop keystone).
The map (MAP.md) is authoritative over memory of the conversation; a slate is authoritative for session continuity. Drift between them is named, not silently reconciled.
A slate that names a file/flag/ticket is a pointer to verify (Law 3), not a proof.
